No 'CONSUMED (or MATCHED)' language remains in hook_changes or any implementation-guidance field.

Won't Have clause now contains an explicit NOTE: hooks/stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh performs a legitimate turn-end expiry rm -f on the grant file — this is a different semantic (expired unconsumed grant cleanup, not tool-execution consumption), is preserved unchanged, and is explicitly out of scope for this cycle. AC4 also contains an explicit NOTE naming stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh, describing its line 25 rm -f as expiry cleanup, and stating it does not violate AC4 because AC4 scopes to tool-execution consumption unlink only. Verified by reading stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh:22-25.

AC7 now includes: AND the heredoc does NOT contain any of the following identifiers: json.load, fcntl.flock, claude-bash-allowlist-, flag_path, flag_file, safe_search — the shim body is limited to: sys.path.insert, import match_grant_for_bash_command from lib.allowlist, call it with (cmd, sid), and print the CONSUMED protocol on match result. This is a full prohibited-identifier list covering the original concern and additional duplication markers.

_match_loaded_grant is now explicitly named in M2 with full signature: _match_loaded_grant(grant: dict, candidates: list[str], literal_policy: str, regex_timeout: int | None) -> MatchResult | None. AC1 requires it by name and signature. Technical Hints section explicitly documents the call hierarchy: read_grant -> _load_and_match -> _match_loaded_grant; read_grant_for_git_command -> _load_and_match -> _match_loaded_grant; match_grant_for_bash_command -> (own NB lock) -> _match_loaded_grant directly.

Actual file at line 21: if [ "$_WG_IS_SUB" != "1" ]; then wraps the ENTIRE /do+/allow block (lines 21-55). M6 replaces lines 32-54 which are INSIDE that gated block. IS_SUBAGENT = '1' causes the entire block to be skipped. The M6 replacement preserves this invariant because it stays inside the same outer if.

lib/allowlist.py uses pattern == tool_name or pattern in tool_name. With tool_name = 'Write', this is: pattern == 'Write' or pattern in 'Write'. The existing write-guard Python block at file line 45 uses exactly the same literal branch. Regex branch in both cases uses re.search(pattern, 'Write'). The consolidation is semantics-preserving.

No sixth independent reader.
No 'CONSUMED (or MATCHED)' language in any implementation-guidance field. context constraints and AC7 both say CONSUMED. Won't Have explicitly prohibits renaming to MATCHED.

finding_1_hooks_dir_empty_string_in_cont.rationale: Context JSON line 256 sys_path_note says 'HOOKS_DIR=""; export HOOKS_DIR' (empty string). The ticket (authoritative document) at lines 302-303 and AC7 line 234 both correctly specify HOOKS_DIR=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")"). A dev reading the ticket as primary spec gets the correct pattern. The context JSON's sys_path_note is supplementary lower-authority guidance. Codex classified this as BLOCKER; QA reclassifies to in_scope_minor because the ticket is correct and authoritative. Same finding classified in_scope_minor/observation_only in iteration 3 Codex finding_3. Documentation quality gap noted for BA awareness.

finding_2_bash_wrapper_flag_file_lines_1.rationale: Current pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 155-156 compute the grant path (flag_file variable) and do a -f existence check outside the heredoc. After M5 replaces lines 163-323, these bash lines remain. M5/AC7 scope the ban to inside the Python heredoc only. R2 says 'grant-file open/flock/JSON-load/match lives only in lib'. Lines 155-156 are bash-level path computation and filesystem existence pre-check, NOT open/flock/JSON-load/match. These are a lightweight optimization guard and do not duplicate the grant-read logic. Outside AC7's heredoc-body ban.

finding_3_ac8_omits_write_guard_behavior.rationale: AC8 covers git-guard, bash-safety, posttool, and lib for IS_SUBAGENT behavioral tests, but not pretool-write-guard.sh. AC11 covers write-guard IS_SUBAGENT statically. The IS_SUBAGENT invariant is specified; absence of a runtime behavioral test is a test-coverage gap, not a blocking spec defect. Write-guard IS_SUBAGENT check is the outermost gate wrapping the entire /allow block (lines 21-55) — any M6 replacement inside those lines inherits this protection structurally.

finding_4_ac4_does_not_gate_consume_gran.rationale: AC4 counts os.unlink in lib but does not assert that only posttool-allowlist-consume.py may import/call consume_grant_for_posttool. Codex proposes adding a grep-based AC to restrict callers. Valid hardening gap, but not a blocking spec defect: (a) scope names only 5 files — no other hook would accidentally import a non-existent function; (b) the spec explicitly says posttool-only caller; (c) AC6 requires posttool to import it correctly. Noted for a future hardening cycle.

finding_5_context_json_missing_m6_hook_c.rationale: context JSON hook_changes array (lines 248-262) lists git, bash-safety, posttool but not pretool-write-guard.sh. Ticket M6/AC11 fully specify the change. scope.included and files-to-modify both include pretool-write-guard.sh. Documentation gap, not a blocking spec defect.

finding_6_remaining_32_53_references_not.rationale: Codex independently confirmed: remaining 32-53 references in historical/evidence sections are not blockers. Binding prescription locations all correctly say 32-54.

qa.overall_assessment: Iteration 4: PASS. All 6 prior objections (NEW-1 through NEW-6) correctly resolved. NEW-6 (M6 line range off-by-one) is fully fixed: all four binding prescription locations in the ticket now say '32-54', AC11 has the bash -n syntax gate, and pretool-write-guard.sh passes bash -n exit 0. Codex adversarial review (gpt-5.5 xhigh reasoning) found no new blocking issues. Codex raised 5 findings — all classified as in_scope_minor or out_of_scope observations: (1) HOOKS_DIR empty-string in context JSON sys_path_note — ticket is authoritative and correct; (2) bash wrapper lines 155-156 path/existence-check surviving M5 — not open/flock/match, outside AC7 scope; (3) AC8 missing write-guard IS_SUBAGENT behavioral test — covered statically by AC11; (4) AC4 not restricting who calls consume_grant_for_posttool — spec names posttool-only caller, narrow scope; (5) context JSON missing M6 hook_changes entry — ticket fully covers M6. The spec is sound, internally consistent on the binding implementation prescription, and ready for dev.
qa.summary: PASS (iteration 4) — 0 new objections. All 6 prior objections resolved. NEW-6 line range fix verified across all 4 binding locations. bash -n gate verified. Codex raised no new blockers (all findings in_scope_minor or out_of_scope). Spec ready for dev.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh reasoning) reviewed the spec and found 6 items: (1) BLOCKER (Codex) — context JSON line 256 HOOKS_DIR='' empty-string placeholder vs ticket's correct $(cd ...) pattern; (2) MAJOR — bash wrapper lines 155-156 (flag_file path + -f check) survive M5 replacement; (3) MAJOR — AC8 omits write-guard IS_SUBAGENT behavioral test; (4) MAJOR — AC4 does not restrict which hooks may call consume_grant_for_posttool; (5) MINOR — context JSON missing M6 hook_changes entry; (6) OBSERVATION_ONLY — remaining 32-53 references are not blockers. Codex overall verdict: would not PASS due to HOOKS_DIR contradiction. QA disagrees on severity of finding 1: ticket is authoritative and correct.
